notes: Score holding at 3.6. The CSS syntax error I flagged in review #8 is STILL there — that's the most embarrassing one, it's literally a broken selector. The office view remains the star of the show. New task tags and error halo from last cycle landed well. But we keep accumulating tech debt without fixing the fundamentals. Time to fix bugs before adding features.

suggestions.title: CSS syntax error — orphaned .skeleton block in App.css STILL UNFIXED
suggestions.description: This has been flagged since review #8 and is still broken. Around line 370 of App.css, there's a bare property block ('background: var(--surface2); border-radius: var(--radius); animation: shimmer...') that's missing its '.skeleton' selector. This is a CSS parse error that will cause any styles defined after it to potentially break. Fix: add '.skeleton {' before the orphaned properties. This is a 10-second fix. Please do it.

suggestions.title: Office tab has no loading state
suggestions.description: Dashboard tab has LoadingSkeleton but Office tab renders an empty floor with just Bloberto while data fetches. First-time visitors see a sad empty office. The isLoading state exists in App.jsx but isn't passed to or used by Office.jsx. Add a simple 'Loading team...' overlay or reuse the skeleton pattern.

suggestions.title: Tab and filter buttons lack focus-visible styles
suggestions.description: .tab-toggle button and .activity-filter-bar button have :hover styles but no :focus-visible outlines. Keyboard-only users can't see what's focused. Add: '.tab-toggle button:focus-visible, .activity-filter-bar button:focus-visible { outline: 2px solid var(--accent); outline-offset: 2px; }'. Two lines of CSS.

suggestions.title: No immediate sync after idle timeout recovery
suggestions.description: When IDLE_TIMEOUT (10 min) stops polling and the user returns, they have to wait up to 30s for the next interval tick to get fresh data. Add: check in the activity event listeners if lastActivity was beyond IDLE_TIMEOUT and if so, call syncFromGitHub() immediately.

suggestions.title: seenIdsRef never resets — 'new' animation only fires once per session
suggestions.description: seenIdsRef tracks all worker IDs ever seen in the session. If a worker clocks out and clocks back in, they won't get the green pulse animation because their ID is already in seenIdsRef. For a live dashboard where workers come and go repeatedly, this means returning workers look like they were always there. Consider resetting or using a time-based approach.

suggestions.title: Add a Team/Roster tab or section
suggestions.description: Roster data is fetched and used for ghost placement but there's no way to browse the full team. A 'Team' tab or section showing all members with roles, status (active/off), and hire dates would be useful. The data is already there — just needs a UI.

suggestions.title: prevActiveWorkersRef diffing runs on every render
suggestions.description: The useEffect that diffs activeWorkers runs every time allWorkers changes, but activeWorkers is recalculated on every render (no useMemo). This means the diff logic and all the timer management runs more than necessary. Wrap activeWorkers in useMemo to prevent unnecessary diff cycles.

suggestions.title: Rotate footer quotes like the Bloberto speech bubbles
suggestions.description: Footer permanently shows 'If it compiles, ship it.' — would be more engaging to rotate through quotes on each page load. The MANAGER_QUOTES array already exists in Office.jsx — export it or duplicate a few for the footer.

suggestions.title: Suggestion history is lost — each review overwrites
suggestions.description: suggestions.json gets overwritten every review. No way to track score trends over time. Consider appending reviews to an array or using timestamped filenames. This would let us build a score chart on the dashboard eventually.

suggestions.title: Idle workers wander through desks — path collision
suggestions.description: The wander keyframe paths (wander-1 through wander-4) can overlap with desk positions, making idle workers appear to walk through furniture. Constrain wander paths to the lower 20% of the floor (y: 78-90%) and the coffee corner area to avoid visual clipping with desks.
